title: Bootstrap project: CLAUDE.md, README, claude skills
description:
Bootstrap the pebblr project with foundational documentation files. This is a custom self-hosted CRM for field sales lead management.

DELIVERABLES:
1. CLAUDE.md - Comprehensive project conventions and context for Claude Code sessions
2. README.md - Project overview, architecture decisions, and setup instructions
3. .claude/commands/ skills - Any useful claude skills for this stage

STRUCTURED REQUIREMENTS TO ENCODE:

Product:
- Custom self-hosted CRM for field sales/lead tracking
- Customers create, assign, track leads for reps who visit customer locations
- Multiple customer types
- Self-hosted per-customer in Kubernetes

Architecture:
- Backend: Go (clean REST API, future mobile apps)
- Frontend: TypeScript, Web Components standard
- UI inspired by Twenty CRM (github.com/twentyhq/twenty)
- Live updates nice-to-have (SSE/WebSocket)
- Per-row/document RBAC (users only see their assigned leads)
- Feature flags (future centralized licensing server)

Auth & Security:
- Azure AD (Entra ID) for login
- Secrets via file mounts, NOT env vars
- External Secrets Operator + Azure KeyVault

Infrastructure:
- AKS deployment target
- Helm 4 for K8s deployments
- Local dev: Kind cluster on Linux/WSL, federated credentials from test Azure tenant, managed identity
- No native Windows support needed

Dev Practices:
- TDD approach
- CI/CD pipelines call Makefile targets only
- Makefile targets are one-liners or call bash scripts
- TypeScript and Go are the languages

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- CLAUDE.md captures all conventions, tech choices, and project context
- README covers project purpose, architecture overview, tech stack, local dev setup outline
- Claude skills created if any make sense at this bootstrap stage
- Files are well-structured and production-quality

title: Add missing conventions to CLAUDE.md and create new-component skill
description:
The bootstrap CLAUDE.md and README are merged but missing several conventions from the original spec:

1. Web Component pbl- prefix convention (all components use pbl- prefix, e.g. <pbl-lead-card>)
2. API versioning: /api/v1/...
3. Structured error responses: {"error": {"code": "...", "message": "..."}}
4. Go error wrapping: fmt.Errorf("doing X: %w", err)
5. Dependency injection: no global state, pass deps via constructors
6. List endpoint pagination and filtering convention
7. Project layout: change frontend/ to web/ and charts/ to deploy/helm/ (in CLAUDE.md and README.md)
8. Add .claude/commands/new-component.md scaffolding skill for Web Components with pbl- prefix

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- CLAUDE.md updated with all 7 convention additions
- README.md project layout fixed (web/ not frontend/, deploy/helm/ not charts/)
- new-component.md skill created in .claude/commands/
- All changes committed
closeReason: Added 7 missing conventions to CLAUDE.md (pbl- prefix, API versioning, structured errors, Go error wrapping, DI, pagination/filtering, layout fix web/ and deploy/helm/), updated README.md layout references, created .claude/commands/new-component.md scaffolding skill

title: Switch frontend from Web Components to React + TypeScript
description:
Update all project documentation to replace Web Components with React + TypeScript.

CHANGES NEEDED:

CLAUDE.md:
- Replace Web Components references with React + TypeScript (strict mode)
- Remove pbl- prefix convention (no longer needed with React components)
- Add React conventions: functional components, hooks, TanStack Query for server state, TanStack Table for data grids
- Update bundler mention (Vite is the standard React bundler now)
- Update project layout: web/src/components/ still works but components are now React .tsx files
- Keep everything else (Go backend, REST API, secrets, RBAC, Helm, etc.)

README.md:
- Update tech stack table: Frontend row should say React + TypeScript
- Update any Web Components mentions

.claude/commands/new-component.md:
- Rewrite to scaffold a React component (.tsx) instead of a Web Component
- Include a test file scaffold using vitest/react-testing-library

.claude/commands/rbac-check.md and secrets-audit.md:
- Update any Web Components references if present

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Zero mentions of Web Components remain in project docs (except possibly as a historical note)
- React + TypeScript conventions are clear and opinionated
- TanStack Query and TanStack Table mentioned as standard libraries
- new-component skill scaffolds React .tsx components
- All changes committed

title: Scaffold Go backend: cmd/, internal/, domain types, store interfaces
description:
Scaffold the Go backend project structure for pebblr CRM.

FILES TO CREATE:

go.mod, go.sum (module: github.com/pebblr/pebblr, Go 1.22+)

cmd/api/main.go - minimal main that wires up dependencies and starts HTTP server. Use net/http. Placeholder for now.

internal/api/ - HTTP handlers package
  - router.go: chi or stdlib mux setup, /api/v1/ prefix, middleware chain
  - handlers.go: placeholder handler interfaces
  - middleware.go: auth middleware placeholder, request ID, logging

internal/auth/ - Azure AD OIDC
  - auth.go: Authenticator interface (ValidateToken, GetClaims)
  - claims.go: UserClaims struct (sub, email, roles, teams)
  - middleware.go: HTTP middleware that validates Bearer tokens

internal/domain/ - Business entities
  - lead.go: Lead struct (ID, Title, Description, Status, AssigneeID, TeamID, CustomerID, CustomerType, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt)
  - lead_status.go: LeadStatus enum (new, assigned, in_progress, visited, closed_won, closed_lost)
  - customer.go: Customer struct (ID, Name, Type, Address, etc.)
  - customer_type.go: CustomerType enum (TBD, but the type system)
  - user.go: User struct (ID, ExternalID/AAD OID, Email, Name, Role, TeamIDs)
  - team.go: Team struct (ID, Name, ManagerID)
  - role.go: Role enum (rep, manager, admin) with permission definitions

internal/rbac/ - Row-level access control
  - rbac.go: Enforcer interface with methods like CanViewLead, CanAssignLead, ScopeQuery
  - policy.go: Role-based policy definitions (rep sees own, manager sees team, admin sees all)
  - context.go: Helper to extract user/role from context

internal/events/ - Lead lifecycle events
  - event.go: LeadEvent struct (ID, LeadID, EventType, ActorID, Payload, Timestamp)
  - event_type.go: EventType enum (created, assigned, status_changed, note_added, visited, closed)
  - recorder.go: EventRecorder interface
  - querier.go: EventQuerier interface for telemetry aggregations

internal/metrics/ - Business telemetry
  - metrics.go: MetricsService interface (PipelineStats, RepPerformance, TeamOverview)
  - types.go: Response types for metric endpoints

internal/store/ - PostgreSQL repositories
  - store.go: Store interface aggregating all repositories
  - lead_store.go: LeadRepository interface (CRUD + scoped queries)
  - user_store.go: UserRepository interface
  - team_store.go: TeamRepository interface
  - event_store.go: EventRepository interface
  - postgres/ subdirectory for implementations (can be placeholder files with TODO)

migrations/ (at project root)
  - 001_initial_schema.sql: CREATE TABLE for users, teams, customers, leads, lead_events. Include RLS policies. Use goose or golang-migrate format.

Also UPDATE CLAUDE.md:
- Add PostgreSQL 16 as the database choice
- Add RLS as defense-in-depth for RBAC
- Add lead_events table for event sourcing / telemetry
- Add role model (rep, manager, admin)
- Update internal/ package list to match actual scaffold
- Add database migration convention (golang-migrate, migrations/ directory)

CONVENTIONS:
- All interfaces, no implementations yet (except domain types which are concrete)
- Every package gets a doc.go with package comment
- Test files: create at least one _test.go per package with a placeholder test
- Use context.Context everywhere
- Errors wrapped with fmt.Errorf
- No third-party deps except: chi (router), pgx (postgres driver), golang-migrate

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- go mod tidy succeeds
- go build ./... succeeds (all packages compile)
- go vet ./... passes
- Project structure matches CLAUDE.md layout
- All domain types defined with proper fields
- All interfaces defined with proper method signatures
- Migration SQL is valid and includes RLS policies
closeReason: Go backend scaffold complete. Created: go.mod, cmd/api + cmd/migrate, internal/{domain,rbac,api,auth,events,metrics,store,store/postgres}, migrations/001_initial_schema.sql (with RLS), CLAUDE.md updated. All quality gates pass (go build, go vet, go test). Branch: overstory/go-foundation-builder/pebblr-5b59 sent as merge_ready.

title: Scaffold React frontend: Vite, TanStack Query/Table/Router, app shell
description:
Scaffold the React + TypeScript frontend for pebblr CRM in web/.

FILES TO CREATE:

web/package.json - dependencies:
  - react, react-dom
  - @tanstack/react-query, @tanstack/react-table, @tanstack/react-router
  - typescript, vite, @vitejs/plugin-react
  - vitest, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/jest-dom, jsdom
  - eslint, prettier (basic config)

web/tsconfig.json - strict: true, jsx: react-jsx, paths configured

web/vite.config.ts - React plugin, proxy /api to localhost:8080 (Go API server), build output to dist/

web/index.html - minimal HTML shell, mount point for React

web/src/main.tsx - React app entry, QueryClientProvider, RouterProvider

web/src/App.tsx - App shell with TanStack Router setup

web/src/routes/ - TanStack Router file-based or manual routes:
  - __root.tsx: root layout (nav shell, sidebar placeholder)
  - index.tsx: dashboard/home (placeholder)
  - leads/index.tsx: leads list view (placeholder with TanStack Table setup)
  - leads/.tsx: single lead view (placeholder)

web/src/components/ - shared components:
  - Layout.tsx: app layout with sidebar nav and main content area
  - DataTable.tsx: generic TanStack Table wrapper component
  - LoadingSpinner.tsx: loading state component

web/src/services/ - API client layer:
  - api.ts: base fetch wrapper (adds auth headers, handles errors per API convention)
  - leads.ts: lead CRUD functions + TanStack Query hooks (useLeads, useLead, useCreateLead, etc.)
  - auth.ts: Azure AD MSAL integration placeholder

web/src/types/ - shared TypeScript types:
  - lead.ts: Lead, LeadStatus, CreateLeadInput types (mirror Go domain)
  - user.ts: User, Role types
  - api.ts: ApiError type matching backend {error: {code, message}} format

web/src/styles/ - CSS:
  - global.css: CSS reset, CSS custom properties for theming (inspired by Twenty CRM: clean, dense, neutral palette)
  - layout.css: sidebar + main content grid

web/src/test/ - test setup:
  - setup.ts: vitest setup file (testing-library matchers)

web/vitest.config.ts - vitest config with jsdom environment

web/.eslintrc.cjs - ESLint config for React + TypeScript

CONVENTIONS:
- Functional components only, hooks only
- TanStack Query for ALL server state (no raw fetch in components)
- TanStack Table for ALL tabular data
- TanStack Router for routing
- Strict TypeScript, no any
- CSS modules or plain CSS (no Tailwind, no CSS-in-JS)
- Test files colocated: Component.test.tsx next to Component.tsx

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- bun install succeeds (or npm install)
- bun run typecheck passes (tsc --noEmit)
- bun run lint passes
- bun run test passes (placeholder tests)
- bun run dev starts Vite dev server
- bun run build produces dist/ output
- App shell renders with sidebar layout and router
- Leads list page shows empty TanStack Table ready for data
closeReason: Merged foundation-builder and appshell-builder branches — React frontend scaffold complete

title: Scaffold DevOps: Makefile, Dockerfiles, Helm chart, Kind setup, scripts
description:
Scaffold the DevOps/infrastructure layer for pebblr CRM.

FILES TO CREATE:

Makefile (project root) - ALL CI/CD targets. Each target is a one-liner or delegates to scripts/:
  - make build: build Go binary + React frontend
  - make test: run Go tests + frontend tests
  - make lint: run Go linter (golangci-lint) + ESLint
  - make typecheck: tsc --noEmit in web/
  - make dev: start local dev environment (Kind cluster + port-forwards + Vite dev server)
  - make dev-api: run Go API server locally with hot reload (air or go run)
  - make dev-web: run Vite dev server
  - make docker-build: build Docker images
  - make cluster-up: create Kind cluster
  - make cluster-down: destroy Kind cluster
  - make secrets-up: apply ESO + test secrets to Kind cluster
  - make deploy: helm install to local cluster
  - make migrate: run database migrations
  - make clean: clean build artifacts

Dockerfile (project root) - multi-stage build:
  - Stage 1: Go builder (golang:1.22-alpine, build cmd/api)
  - Stage 2: Frontend builder (node:20-alpine, build web/)
  - Stage 3: Runtime (alpine:3.19, copy Go binary + static frontend files)
  - Secrets read from file mounts, not ENV
  - Non-root user

Dockerfile.migrate - migration runner image (optional, could be same binary with subcommand)

deploy/helm/pebblr/ - Helm 4 chart:
  - Chart.yaml: name pebblr, version 0.1.0
  - values.yaml: default values (image, replicas, resources, secret paths, postgres connection)
  - templates/deployment.yaml: API server deployment with secret volume mounts, probes, resource limits
  - templates/service.yaml: ClusterIP service
  - templates/ingress.yaml: ingress template (optional, disabled by default)
  - templates/configmap.yaml: non-secret configuration
  - templates/externalsecret.yaml: ExternalSecret CRD for KeyVault secrets
  - templates/migration-job.yaml: Job that runs migrations before deployment
  - templates/serviceaccount.yaml: SA with Azure workload identity annotation
  - templates/_helpers.tpl: standard Helm helpers
  - templates/NOTES.txt: post-install notes

deploy/kind/ - Kind cluster config:
  - kind-config.yaml: Kind cluster config (extra port mappings for API, ingress)

scripts/ - bash scripts called by Makefile:
  - cluster-up.sh: create Kind cluster, install ESO, apply base config
  - cluster-down.sh: destroy Kind cluster
  - secrets-up.sh: apply ExternalSecret resources for test tenant
  - dev.sh: start full local dev stack (postgres in cluster, API, Vite)
  - docker-build.sh: build and tag Docker images
  - migrate.sh: run migrations against local or remote DB

.github/workflows/ (optional, basic):
  - ci.yml: on push/PR, run make test, make lint, make typecheck

docker-compose.yml (project root) - for running postgres locally outside Kind if needed:
  - postgres:16 with volume mount, test credentials via file mounts

.dockerignore - exclude .git, node_modules, .overstory, etc.
.gitignore - update with Go, Node, Docker, Helm ignores

CONVENTIONS:
- Makefile targets are one-liners or call scripts/
- All secrets via file mounts in Helm templates (no env vars for secrets)
- Helm values use secretMountPath pattern
- Docker images are minimal (alpine-based)
- Scripts are bash, set -euo pipefail, have usage comments
- Kind cluster config mirrors prod AKS where possible

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- make help (or similar) lists available targets
- Makefile syntax is valid (make -n succeeds for all targets)
- Dockerfile builds successfully (docker build .)
- Helm chart lints cleanly (helm lint deploy/helm/pebblr)
- Kind config is valid YAML
- All scripts have execute permission and shebang
- .gitignore covers Go, Node, Docker, Helm artifacts
- No secrets in env vars anywhere in Helm templates
closeReason: Scaffolded full DevOps layer: Makefile (14 targets), Dockerfile (multi-stage), Dockerfile.migrate, Helm 4 chart (11 templates with ESO, probes, resource limits, migration hook), Kind config, 7 bash scripts, CI workflow, docker-compose, .dockerignore, .gitignore. 27 files total. merge_ready sent for makefile-builder branch.

title: Add Go linting with good defaults and enforce t.Parallel
description: Set up golangci-lint with good defaults. Add tparallel linter to enforce t.Parallel() in all tests. Update existing tests to call t.Parallel(). Wire into Makefile lint target.
closeReason: Merged — golangci-lint v2 config with tparallel/paralleltest, all tests use t.Parallel()

title: Overhaul DevOps: Skaffold, simplified Makefile, AKS guards, Dependabot
description: Replace docker-build/deploy scripts with Skaffold. Simplify Makefile to cluster-up (recreate Kind + install deps) and deploy (skaffold). Add AKS safety guards. Remove redundant scripts. Pin dependency versions. Add Dependabot + GH Action.
closeReason: DevOps overhaul complete: added skaffold.yaml (v4beta11, Kind profile), rewrote Makefile (AKS guard, pinned ESO 0.12.1 + ingress-nginx 4.12.1, cluster-up recreates Kind), deleted 7 redundant scripts, added Dependabot config (gomod/npm/github-actions), Dependabot auto-merge workflow (patch+minor), updated CI with pinned versions + golangci-lint-action.
